title: Using pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) is security-sensitive
description: Using pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweUsing pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) is security-sensitive. For example, it has led in the past to the following vulnerabilities:CVE-2013-6386CVE-2006-3419CVE-2008-4102When software generates predictable values in a context requiring unpredictability, it may be possible for an attacker to guess the next value that will be generated, and use this guess to impersonate another user or access sensitive information.As theMath.random()function relies on a weak pseudorandom number generator, this function should not be used for security-critical applications or for protecting sensitive data. In such context, a cryptographically strong pseudorandom number
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
